asset_name: Student Reflector Core Sheet — idle, card ready, cast, hit, victory
shared_properties: The student keeps a readable academy uniform silhouette, one raised card hand, and a defensive off-hand stance.
All states fit the documented combat footprint and align to a shared floor contact point.
Card-reflection poses need clear arm silhouettes so the timing interaction reads without text.
differences: This task covers the playable student avatar only.
Enemy champions, projectile effects, and card-face art stay in separate files.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Moodboard for costume material contrast, gold academy trims, and spell-lit shading.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_player_silhouette
reference_images.purpose: Player silhouette board for preserving a readable combatant pose with a visible card hand and shoulder line.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load assets/art/reflect_academy/trial_2/character_student_reflector_core_sheet.png during DuelLoading and store the atlas under character_student_reflector_core_sheet.
runtime_notes: Map the animation states to idle, card_ready, reflect_cast, damage_react, and victory_loop ranges.
Keep the combat root at the character feet so the student locks to the player podium without per-state offset fixes.
Allow the reflect_cast state to layer the telegraph and projectile effects in front of the raised card hand.

context.usage: Playable student sheet used throughout combat, including idle reads, reaction frames, and successful reflection poses.

shape.silhouette: Slim academy student with capelet, raised card hand, staff-brace off hand, and forward defensive stance.

expression_notes: Idle frames show focused concentration rather than aggression.
Reflect cast frames widen the stance and flare the card-hand glow toward the lane.
Damage react frames tilt the torso back without moving the feet off the podium anchor.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 480x720px with sixteen 120x180 frames and no padding.
All frames share one consistent floor contact point so state swaps do not make the student slide.
Card hand, coat trim, and face silhouette remain readable at gameplay scale over the academy backdrop.
Reflect cast frames visibly communicate a defensive counter motion rather than a direct attack swing.
